spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates the current-document, document-URL, and document-URL-plus-manifest-id navigator.install call shapes with generated calls and real run buttons.

interactive.rationale: Inputs, per-signature run buttons, runtime probes, and fallback branch controls update live call previews, outcomes, and logs rather than showing static code cards.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and controls match the feature framing that Web Install API experiments with three signatures in parallel.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo visibly covers missing API, invalid manifest or id inputs, user cancellation via AbortError, and non-installable context fallback handling.

references.rationale: The page includes the required https://chromestatus.com/feature/5183481574850560 link.

design_system.rationale: Inline CSS uses shared variables, button background overrides pair color values, and the output is an interactive surface instead of a static code-card page.

summary: The ChromeStatus link gap and the edge-case branch gap are addressed. The concept now generates all three Web Install API call shapes live, attempts the real API when available, and routes key failures through visible fallback outcomes.
